verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface during epididymal transit. It becomes stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane (postacrosomal / equatorial segment) in a wash-resistant manner, enabling its role in sperm-egg fusion. This constitutes a tissue-restricted surface display confined to mature sperm and the epididymal compartment. The protein reaches the outer leaflet of the sperm PM via stable post-translational surface attachment in this narrow germline/gamete lineage, qualifying as contextual / tissue_restricted_surface rather than a broadly surface-accessible protein.
